chunk_summary: This section outlines workflows for calculating z-scores and documenting growth charts for undernutrition screening in children aged 0–59 months and 5–19 years, emphasizing WHO standards and tools like WHO Anthro and AnthroPlus.
learning_objectives: Understand the workflow for calculating z-scores and documenting growth charts in undernutrition screening.
Learn to use WHO Child Growth Standards and WHO Anthro/AnthroPlus tools for accurate classification.
Identify key indicators (e.g., WHZ, WLZ, HAZ, WAZ, BAZ) and their interpretation for different age groups.
Develop strategies to ensure continuity of care using caregiver-held growth charts.
key_facts: For children aged 0–59 months, use WHO Child Growth Standards and tools like WHO Anthro to calculate WHZ/WLZ, HAZ, and WAZ.
For ages 5–19 years, use the WHO 2007 reference and interpret thinness using BAZ; avoid weight-for-age as the primary indicator.
A one-page proforma should be used for children with any index < -2 SD to ensure comprehensive assessment.
Linear growth should be reassessed over months, not days, to avoid unnecessary re-measurements.
Standardized printed charts can be used when software is unavailable, but consistent references must be maintained across visits.
reference_quote: For children 0–59 months, use the WHO Child Growth Standards and a tool that outputs z-scores consistently (e.g., WHO Anthro or an equivalent charting system).
document_key_topics: Use of WHO Child Growth Standards and tools
Continuity of care with growth charts
